data.title: NeOnc files for direct listing in wake of pulled IPO
data.description:
Brain cancer drug developer NeOnc Technologies (NASDAQ:NTHI) plans to go public via a direct listing of up to 2.1M of its common shares.

The company will not receive proceeds from the offering, as the shares will be sold by registered stockholders. NeoOnc (NASDAQ:NTHI) hopes to list its shares on Nasdaq under the symbol NTHI. RBW Capital Partners is serving as financial advisor, according to an SEC filing.

NeOnc (NTHI) didn't disclose a reference price for the stock, but said it has private placement agreements to sell 625K of its common shares for $16 per share prior to the listing.

The company filed for a traditional IPO in December 2023, seeking around $50M, but withdrew the filing in June 2024.

Based in Westlake Village, Calif., NeOnc's (NTHI) lead drug candidate is NEO100, a purified form of perillyl alcohol which is delivered intranasally. The product is currently in Phase 2 testing for the treatment of malignant glioma and meningioma.

data.title: Acelyrin spikes on upcoming mid-stage data update on eye disease drug
data.description:
Acelyrin (NASDAQ:SLRN) shares traded higher on Friday after the company scheduled an investor meeting for next week to discuss updated data from a Phase 2 trial for lonigutamab, an injectable targeted at thyroid eye disease (TED).

At the virtual event, the company will also disclose a design for its Phase 3 LONGITUDE program for the drug, developed using FDA feedback received at a recent end-of-Phase 2 meeting, the Agoura Hills, California-based biotech said in a press release late Thursday.

The company added that Acelyrin (NASDAQ:SLRN) CEO Mina Kim, medical chief Shep Mpofu, and commercial chief Ken Loc will attend the event scheduled for Jan. 6 at 4:30 PM ET.

Drs. Andrea Kossler and Prem Subramanian, members of the biotech’s scientific and patient advisory group and academics of the Stanford University School of Medicine and University of Colorado School of Medicine, respectively, will also join the event.

data.title: Evercore ISI predicts Tech will lead the S&P 500 to another 20% upside year in 2025
data.description:
Evercore ISI expressed on Friday that Wall Street will likely see more of what it experienced in 2024 as the investment institution stated “Fill IT up again” in 2025 indicating its positive view on “IT.”

Evercore ISI echoed that “the bull market in the S&P remains strong” and is intact as the firm speculates “another 22% upside to 7,200 in 2025.” For reference, as 2025 is now just underway, the benchmark index sits at 5,940.

Supporting its view of a strong market is the Info Tech sector of the economy, as Evercore ISI believes tech will lead markets higher once again this year. Furthermore, from a sub-industry perspective both semiconductors and software were highlighted.

Regarding the semiconductor space, the investment bank added “semis are coiled for a major move higher,” and stressed its positions in high conviction buys: Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM).

In terms of the software industry, the firm said “Software will outperform as IT did in ’24” and highlighted shares of Microsoft (MSFT).

As for the magnificent seven names: (AAPL), (AMZN), (GOOG), (GOOGL), (META), (MSFT), (NVDA), and (TSLA), the financial firm stated: “Buy them all.”

With regards to the S&P 500 (SP500), see the below chart that Evercore ISI provided:

data.title: 2024 recap: Small caps have now lagged every year since 2017 – BofA
data.description:
Small caps (NYSEARCA:IWM) had the worst upside in 2024.

The Russell 2000 (IWM) was up 11.5% on a total return basis. The Russell MidCap (IWR), on the other hand, was up 15.3%, and the Russell 1000 (IWB) was up 24.5%.

“Small caps (IWM) have now lagged large in every calendar year since 2017, with 2024’s 13 percentage points of underperformance the largest of any year since 1998,” said Savita Subramanian, head of U.S. Equity Strategy and U.S. Quantitative Strategy at BofA, in a note.

Technology (PSCT) was the best-performing small cap sector, up 25%; and energy (PSCE) was the worst-performing, down 3%.

Also, in December, small caps (IWM) sold off 8.3%, particularly after the Federal Reserve meeting, with all sectors down, underperforming mid-caps -7.0%, and large-caps -2.8%, said Subramanian.

“We prefer mid-caps (IWR) over small, and mega in 2025.”

data.title: Skechers running towards $10B in revenue by FY26 - analyst
data.description:
Skechers (NYSE:SKX) brand name and revenue growth potential is underestimated by investors, and the company’s revenue trajectory will secure its status as the third-largest footwear company in the world, UBS analyst Jay Sole says, reiterating his Buy rating on the stock and a price target that assumes a 37% upside.

“We believe Skechers is on track to deliver $10B in revenue by 2026,” Sole predicts, citing the findings from UBS Evidence Labs 10th annual global athletic wear survey. Skechers’ (SKX) survey results were among the best of all brands including the 5th highest Net Promotor score, a Loyalty rate that is second only to Nike (NKE), and a Brand Purchase Intention ranking (which measures the brands consumers plan to purchase) that tops Air Jordan, Asics, and Puma.

For its most recently reported quarter, Skechers (SKX) broke its own quarterly sales records with $2.3B in Q3 sales, fueled by a 20% increase in wholesale sales, and 10% gain in DTC sales. And while the company warned that lower average selling prices adversely impacted profit margins, Sole anticipates profits to accelerate over the next five years, leading to price/earnings expansion.

For Q4, UBS expects Skechers (SKX) to earn a profit of $0.75 per share, down sequentially from Q3, but up 34% year-over-year and a penny better than the consensus estimate. Skechers (SKX) reports Q4 results after the market close on January 30.

UBS’s upbeat outlook and vote of confidence in Skechers (SKX) is giving a lift to the stock, trading another 3.5% higher on Friday.

data.title: Momentum reigned 2024: Buy-rated U.S. stocks with the highest RSI
data.description:
Momentum was the best trading factor group in 2024, up 19%, according to BofA Securities.

Below are buy-rated U.S. stocks by SA Quant Metrics (with Quant ratings of at least 4.5) and the highest relative strength index (or RSI). Values above 70 indicate overbought conditions, and values below 30 indicate oversold conditions. A “balanced market” zone would hoover around 50 levels.

Seeking Alpha's Quant system consistently outperforms the S&P 500 (SP500), driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

data.title: Regeneron expands eye care focus with Oxular buyout
data.description:
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:REGN), the maker of eye disease therapy Eylea, has acquired Oxular, a U.K.-based biotech drug developer focused on ophthalmology.

While neither company formally disclosed the deal, former Oxular CEO Mark Gaffney, who currently leads immunology drug developer Calluna Pharma, shared a LinkedIn post on Thursday announcing the transaction.

“The ophthalmology team at Regeneron is uniquely positioned to maximize the use of Oxular’s proprietary technologies,” Gaffney wrote.

There was no formal announcement on the deal or its financial terms, as the transaction was “non-material” for Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN), Fierce Biotech reported, quoting a company spokesperson.

Oxular, based in Oxford, England, has OXU-001, an experimental therapy for diabetic macular edema, retinal vein occlusion, and uveitis, as its lead asset.

The dexamethasone-based product designed as an eye injection delivered via an ocular device called Oxulumis is currently undergoing mid-stage trials.

“This was a targeted acquisition that provides us with a potential best-in-class delivery device for our future potential ocular gene therapies, with the goal of accelerating the time it will take to reach clinical readiness,” the REGN spokesperson added.

data.title: Portland General Electric cut at UBS on regulatory inflexibility
data.description:
Portland General Electric (NYSE:POR) -0.2% in Friday's trading as UBS downgrades shares to Neutral from Buy with a $47 price target, trimmed from $52, seeing a more balanced risk/reward outlook after recent regulatory decisions.

Portland General (POR) likely will need to cut costs to deliver on fourth quartile 5%-6% earnings per share growth due to the December 20 rate case decision that authorized a low 9.34% return on equity and only 45% of the base request, UBS analyst Gregg Orrill writes.

Affordability concerns limit upside from more investment due to the 33% average residential rate increase from 2023-25 cited in the recent decision, according to Orrill.

The company's service territory has strong industrial demand led by the semiconductor industry and investment potential, but the analyst says third quartile regulation in Oregon and a need for equity both for growth and balance sheet repair will limit EPS growth and returns.

data.title: Growth vs. Value had its best two-year run since 1978 – BofA
data.description:
The Russell 1000 Growth Index (NYSEARCA:IWF) outperformed the Russell 1000 Value Index (IWD) by 63 percentage points over two years, up 90% vs. 27%, after leading by 19 percentage points in 2024, up 33% vs. 14%.

“This represents the biggest two-year outperformance for Growth since the indices’ inception in 1978, topping the 61 percentage points outperformance during the Tech Bubble,” said Savita Subramanian, head of U.S. Equity Strategy and U.S. Quantitative Strategy at BofA, in a note.

In addition, the month of December was a particularly strong month for Growth (IWF), outperforming Value (IWD) by 7.7 percentage points, the best performance since May 2023.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was up 25% in 2024 and up 58% over the past two years. However, it dropped 2.4% in December – its worst month since April.

The equal-weighted S&P 500 (RSP) was up 12% this year, and up 28% over the last two years. It had two of the weakest breadth measures in history, with 30% of stocks outperforming the S&P 500 (SP500) in 2024 and 27% of stocks outperforming in 2023.

data.title: Wall Street shakes off recent weakness, Nasdaq surges more than 1% on tech boost
data.description:
U.S. stocks on Friday surged, as Wall Street finally appeared to shake off the doldrums afflicting it this week. A dull end to 2024 and a dour start to 2025 derailed the Santa Claus rally, and the three major averages were set for weekly losses of nearly 1% each.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +1.00% at 5,927.45 points in midday trade. The gauge was on track to snap a five-day losing streak, its longest such negative run since April.

Meanwhile, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.59% at 42,644.32 points, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.31% at 19,533.98 points.

All 11 S&P sectors were in the green, led by Consumer Discretionary and Utilities.

Technology was +1.3%, as every single member of the Magnificent 7 club climbed - except Apple (AAPL). Reuters earlier reported that sales of foreign-branded smartphones in China tumbled 47.4% Y/Y in November, citing government data. Apple (AAPL) is the largest foreign smartphone manufacturer in China.

Over in the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were mixed. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was up 2 basis points to 4.59%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was marginally down to 4.26%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

Despite the first trading session of the new year ending in the red on Thursday, investors should take heart from the fact that the S&P 500's (SP500) performance on the first trading day of a year has historically been a poor barometer for eventual annual returns. See the chart here.

The spotlight on Friday was captured by a collision between the financial world and politics. Shares of U. S. Steel (X) were -5.6%, after U.S. President Joe Biden officially blocked the proposed $14.9B sale of the iconic 123-year-old American steel giant to Japan's Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY)(OTCPK:NISTF). The President cited national security concerns for his decision.

Beer and alcohol companies also saw some notable stock declines, after the U.S. Surgeon General urged for the addition of cancer warning labels to alcoholic drinks.

Turning to Friday's economic calendar, the Institute for Supply Management said economic activity in the U.S. manufacturing sector improved slightly in December from November, though still staying in contraction for the ninth straight month.

"Few things were as consistent as the ISM Manufacturing Index in the past year. The index has been in contraction for the past nine-straight months and for 25 of the past 26. Uncertainty continues to hold back broad activity, though there are some signs of stabilizing demand amid industries," Wells Fargo's Tim Quinlan said.

"Late last year we heard from manufacturing clients that they were planning for a 2025 similar to 2024, where this persistent uncertainty plagues new investment. But there isn't much sign of this caution, or tariff-anxiety, in today's report. Despite the index remaining in contraction to end the year, the details were fairly positive and consistent with some recent improvement," Quinlan added.
